metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Foundations is a Core Set designed as an MTG introduction featuring half new cards and half reprints, legal for five years in Standard. The set emphasizes straightforward synergies and core MTG concepts with "softer" archetypes than previous sets.

color_notes.{W} White: Excellent removal, three quality fliers at common, strong ground creatures.
color_notes.{R} Red: Best common in the set (Burst Lightning), good high-end options, flat quality distribution.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Big hits available but lacks a solid all-around cast; removal is weak.
color_notes.{B} Black: Strong removal spells and creatures (Infestation Sage, Burglar Rat); weak expensive plays.
color_notes.{G} Green: Worst color relatively. Relies heavily on Llanowar Elves and uncommons.

key_gameplay_notes: Synergy cards like Inspiring Call can function as traps even in their intended archetypes.
Draft card quality in a vacuum — mana curve often matters more than synergy.
Adjust based on actual draft progression, not rigid archetype adherence.
Removal density is crucial given the prevalence of large threats.
Two-card combos (treason + sacrifice, lifegain payoffs) drive most synergy value.

mechanics.name: Lifegain
mechanics.summary: Pairs gain-life triggers with payoffs like Ajani's Pridemate and Exemplar of Light. Core to WB.
mechanics.performance: Reliable when supported; weak as a one-off splash.

mechanics.name: Threshold (Graveyard)
mechanics.summary: UB graveyard-fill payoffs anchored by Dreadwing Scavenger. Cards reward seven-card graveyards.
mechanics.performance: Strong when committed; Dreadwing is a signpost worth building around.

mechanics.name: Raid
mechanics.summary: BR mechanic: bonus effect if you attacked this turn. Pairs with Involuntary Employment for free creatures.

mechanics.name: Spells Matter
mechanics.summary: UR instant/sorcery synergies. Crackling Cyclops and Involuntary Employment support the lane.

mechanics.name: Morbid
mechanics.summary: BG mechanic requiring a creature to die this turn. Requires consistent creature sacrifice outlets and is inconsistent without them.
mechanics.performance: Weakest archetype mechanic; only viable with high density.

mechanics.name: Ramp
mechanics.summary: GU ramp built around Tatyova, Benthic Druid and Evolving Wilds synergy. Enables greedy mana bases.
mechanics.performance: Strong with the signpost; otherwise generic.

tier_1.name: WU Fliers
tier_1.strategy: Efficient creatures plus removal; curve out with fliers, ideally boosted by Empyrean Eagle.

tier_1.name: RG Stompy
tier_1.strategy: Big creatures with Giant Growth effects. Accelerate with Llanowar Elves into oversized threats.

tier_1.name: UB Threshold
tier_1.strategy: Control archetype with Dreadwing Scavenger as signpost. Graveyard-focused threshold payoffs.

tier_2.name: WB Lifegain
tier_2.strategy: Lifegain payoffs: Fiendish Panda, Exemplar of Light, Ajani's Pridemate. Builds critical mass with small lifegain triggers.

tier_2.name: BR Raid
tier_2.strategy: Involuntary Employment synergies — creatures attacking provides fodder value. Cheap aggressive creatures plus removal.

tier_2.name: RW Aggro
tier_2.strategy: Fast starts with combat tricks; pressure-focused strategy.

tier_2.name: GW Midrange
tier_2.strategy: Softer archetype; focuses on card quality over tight synergies.

tier_3.name: GU Ramp
tier_3.strategy: Tatyova, Benthic Druid + Evolving Wilds synergy. Greedy mana base into bombs.

tier_3.name: UR Spells
tier_3.strategy: Instant/sorcery synergies. Crackling Cyclops and Involuntary Employment support.

tier_3.name: BG Morbid
tier_3.strategy: Requires consistent creature sacrifice outlets. Inconsistent mechanic at common.

general_tips: Draft card quality and mana curve before synergy — Foundations rewards solid cards more than gimmicks.
Adjust to draft signals; do not commit to an archetype too early.
Removal density is crucial — large threats appear at every rarity.
Two-card combos (treason + sacrifice, lifegain payoffs) drive most of the synergy value in the set.
Avoid "trap" synergy cards like Inspiring Call unless you have built around them deeply.
